subject: ap-chemistry
title: Chemical Equilibrium & Le Chatelier's Principle
stimulus:
CH OH(g) CO(g) + 2 H (g) ∆H° = 90.0 kJ/mol
3 2 rxn
2. Methanol vapor decomposes to form carbon monoxide gas and hydrogen gas at high temperatures in the presence
of a platinum catalyst, as represented by the balanced chemical equation given.

parts.prompt:
Are the hydrogen atoms oxidized or are they reduced in the forward reaction? Justify your answer in terms of
oxidation numbers.

scoring_points.description: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point

required_elements: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point
alternatives.correct_example: The H atoms are reduced because they change from an oxidation number of +1 to 0.

parts.prompt:

parts.prompt:
The volume of the container is rapidly doubled with no change in temperature. As equilibrium is
re-established, does the number of moles of CH OH(g ) increase, decrease, or remain the same? Justify your
answer by comparing the value of the reaction quotient, Q, with the value of the equilibrium constant, K .
p

scoring_points.description: For a correct comparison of Q and K: 1 point

required_elements: Provide a correct response from the accepted alternatives

scoring_points.description: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point

required_elements: For the correct answer and a valid justification: 1 point
alternatives.correct_example: Decrease. Given that Q < K , the partial pressures (moles) of the products will increase
